title: New funding available for lowland peat soils

content.text: Lowland peat has long supported profitable farming, but current water management practices can lead to soil loss and land subsidence.

From today, farmers and land managers on lowland peat soils can apply for 2 new grants which support farmers and land managers to balance productive businesses with benefits for climate, nature and water.
Every farm is different, and there is no one-size-fits-all approach.
These grants recognise that farming and environmental outcomes can go hand in hand, and that decisions should reflect local business and land needs.
The funding is designed to support practical solutions that work on the ground.
The Lowland Peat Water Implementation Grant
Through the Lowland Peat Water Implementation Grant, £36 million is available to support water management projects on lowland peat soils.
Funding will support water infrastructure projects that help manage water levels across peat landscapes.
This can help to:
Projects can support both sustainable agriculture and wider environmental benefits in lowland peat catchments.
If you already have a water management implementation plan, you can apply for between £100,000 and £2 million. Grants can cover up to 100% of eligible project costs.
The grant scheme is now open for applications and closes at 11.59pm on 18 September 2026.
We expect to award grants in December 2026. Grant-funded projects must be completed by 31 March 2030.
Learn more
The Environment Agency (the organisation delivering the grant) will host a one-hour webinar on 15 July at 10am to explain the grant and the application process.
You will be able to put your questions directly to the team. Register to attend the webinar.
Further details on the Water Implementation Grant scheme can be found on the GOV.UK website.
To request an application pack, email peatwatergrants@environment-agency.gov.uk.

We plan to open a second application window in summer 2028 for applicants who need more time to develop a water management implementation plan. Projects funded through this window must also be completed by 31 March 2030.
The Paludiculture and Wetter Farming Fund
Through the Paludiculture and Wetter Farming Fund, £10 million in grant funding is available to support projects on lowland peat soils.
Funding is available across 2 priority areas:
This can help you:
The fund is open now and closes at 5pm on 21 August 2026.
Learn more
Natural England, the organisation delivering the grant, will host a webinar on 7 July. To register, email PWFF@naturalengland.org.uk.
Full guidance and information on how to apply are available on the Defra eCommercial portal, Atamis where applications can be submitted through from 26 June to 21 August 2026.
Subscribe to the Farming blog

The Farming Blog is the best place to stay up to date with the latest funding opportunities, policy updates and practical information for farmers and land managers in England. Subscribe to the Farming blog to receive an email whenever we publish a new post.
